Title: The emergence and loss of cyclic peptides in Nicotiana illuminate dynamics and mechanisms of plant metabolic evolution

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots in N. attenuata; leaves in heterologous N. benthamiana expression
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: terminal posttranslational cyclization step in precursor peptide maturation
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: BURP-domain peptide macrocyclase / burpitide cyclase precursor
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: NatBURP1-encoded lyciumin core peptide motifs including [QPWGVYSW]
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: autocatalytic copper-dependent macrocyclization of the encoded precursor peptide to generate lyciumins, with the BURP domain functioning as peptide cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes biosynthesis of lyciumins P and Q
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NatBURP1 encodes the lyciumin precursor/cyclase responsible for lyciumin P and Q production in N. attenuata. 2. Transient expression of NatBURP1 in N. benthamiana reconstituted lyciumin P and Q production, validating function. 3. NatBURP1 cyclase shows latent promiscuity by cyclizing nanamin core peptides into nanamin A plus an isomer, suggesting an ancestral promiscuous state.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: plant-specific BURP domain-containing precursor protein; catalytic BURP domain
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: detected from root tissue context via root peptide accumulation/cloning from root cDNA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LC-MS metabolite profiling; heterologous transient expression; site-directed mutagenesis; chimeric residue swapping; MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome context from public datasets used comparatively; accession SRR6918819-SRR6918830 (GSE112526), SRR2913179-SRR2913196 (PRJNA301787)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful macrocyclase platform for engineering branched cyclic peptides and novel peptide scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: less specific than nanamin cyclase; produces nanamin isomer when paired with nanamin core peptides
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Full biosynthesis likely also requires other enzymes such as peptidases and glutaminyl cyclotransferases for leader processing and pyroglutamate formation, but these were not core genes in this study. NatBURP1 shares high identity with LbaLycA and StuBURP and likely reflects ancestral lyciumin state.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT expression vector promoter not specified

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: not detected in N. attenuata due to pseudogenization; produced in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves after pseudogene repair
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: terminal posttranslational cyclization step in precursor peptide maturation
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: BURP-domain peptide macrocyclase / burpitide cyclase precursor
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: encoded nanamin core peptide [QPWGLMY] and engineered variants
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: autocatalytic copper-dependent macrocyclization creating a unique C(sp2)-C(sp3) bond between the tyrosine side chain and glycine alpha-carbon in the encoded heptapeptide, with concomitant pyroglutamate-containing mature product formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: repaired pseudogene 5x_Nna restores nanamin biosynthesis; native pseudogenized ΨNatBURP2 explains nanamin loss in N. attenuata
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ΨNatBURP2 is a pseudogenized paralog of NatBURP1 in N. attenuata. 2. Repairing the frameshift and premature stops generated 5x_Nna, which restored enzymatic production of nanamin A in N. benthamiana. 3. The nanamin cyclase is highly tolerant of many core peptide substitutions, insertions, and some deletions, but requires glycine and tyrosine for cyclization. 4. Swapping five active-site residues can interconvert nanamin and lyciumin cyclase specificities.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: plant-specific BURP domain-containing precursor protein; catalytic BURP domain
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: pseudogene locus in N. attenuata genome; nanamin not detected in tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pseudogene resurrection; transient expression; LC-MS and MS/MS; NMR structural elucidation of product; alanine scan mutagenesis; core peptide insertion/deletion mutagenesis; chimeric active-site swapping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising tool for peptide engineering because it tolerates many sequence variants and forms difficult small-ring C-C macrocycles
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: lost native ability to accept lyciumin core peptide; catalytic efficiency reduced in some mutants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 5x_Nna was synthesized with tandem repeats reduced from 11 to 5 for tractable DNA synthesis. Product nanamin can spontaneously oxidize to nanamin B (methionine sulfoxide). Native chemotype was lost in N. attenuata through pseudogenization.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT expression vector promoter not specified

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots in N. clevelandii; leaves in heterologous N. benthamiana expression
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: terminal posttranslational cyclization step in precursor peptide maturation
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: BURP-domain peptide macrocyclase / burpitide cyclase precursor
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: nanamin A (with observed oxidized nanamin B)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: autocatalytic macrocyclization of encoded nanamin precursor peptide by BURP-domain cyclase to generate nanamin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: confers the wild-type nanamin chemotype in N. clevelandii
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NclBURP2 was identified from N. clevelandii transcriptome as the full-length wild-type nanamin precursor gene. 2. N. clevelandii roots contain nanamin A and oxidized nanamin B. 3. Transient expression of NclBURP2 in N. benthamiana reproduced nanamin production and matched 5x_Nna activity.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: plant-specific BURP domain-containing precursor protein; catalytic BURP domain
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: identified from assembled transcriptome; nanamin detected in root extracts
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq transcriptome assembly; gene cloning; transient expression; LC-MS detection
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key determinant of retained nanamin chemotype
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: de novo transcriptome assembly from public RNA-seq datasets SRR6918819-SRR6918830 and SRR2913179-SRR2913196; Trinity/transXpress
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: wild-type nanamin cyclase source for peptide engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents unequivocally characterized wild-type nanamin precursor gene from N. clevelandii.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT expression vector promoter not specified

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: terminal posttranslational cyclization step in precursor peptide maturation
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: BURP-domain peptide macrocyclase / burpitide cyclase precursor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: inferred BURP-domain precursor/cyclase for lyciumin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with lyciumin chemotype retained in N. clevelandii
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NclBURP1 was identified from the N. clevelandii transcriptome as the full-length lyciumin precursor gene. 2. N. clevelandii roots contain lyciumins, consistent with retention of this gene.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: plant-specific BURP domain-containing precursor protein; catalytic BURP domain
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: identified from assembled transcriptome; lyciumins detected in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq transcriptome assembly; correlation with metabolite detection
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: de novo transcriptome assembly from public RNA-seq datasets SRR6918819-SRR6918830 and SRR2913179-SRR2913196
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential source of lyciumin cyclase for peptide engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Less directly functionally tested than NatBURP1/NclBURP2 in this paper, but identified as a retained full-length lyciumin precursor gene in a core comparative result.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: terminal posttranslational cyclization step in precursor peptide maturation
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: BURP-domain peptide macrocyclase / burpitide cyclase precursor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: inferred BURP-domain precursor/cyclase for lyciumin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with lyciumin chemotype retained in N. pauciflora
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NpaBURP1 was identified from the N. pauciflora transcriptome as a lyciumin precursor gene. 2. N. pauciflora contains lyciumins, consistent with this gene.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: plant-specific BURP domain-containing precursor protein; catalytic BURP domain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq transcriptome assembly; metabolite detection correlation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: de novo transcriptome assembly from public RNA-seq datasets SRR6918819-SRR6918830 and SRR2913179-SRR2913196
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential source of lyciumin pathway engineering component
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The full-length nanamin precursor gene from N. pauciflora could not be reconstructed due to insufficient RNA-seq coverage, but sequence reads suggest its presence.
